title: convert_uuid raises AttributeError on non-string input
body:
convert_uuid() in radicli/util.py calls UUID(value) directly. When value is already a UUID object (e.g. passed programmatically), it raises AttributeError instead of returning it unchanged.



Expected: return the UUID unchanged if already a UUID instance.
Fix: add isinstance(value, UUID) early-return at the top of convert_uuid().
Add regression test in radicli/tests/test_util.py.
